messages.content:
I can’t run tasks from here, but I can tell you exactly how to run “load test task 1” and what to provide if you want me to help interpret the results.

Quick reasoning: I don’t have execution access, so you must run the test locally or on CI and paste the output/logs here for analysis.

What to do next (pick the section that matches your tool):

1) If this is a k6 test
- Ensure k6 is installed: (mac/linux) brew install k6 or download from k6.io.
- Run: k6 run path/to/task1.js
- Optional: k6 run --out json=task1-result.json path/to/task1.js to save machine-readable results.

2) If this is a Locust test
- Ensure locust is installed: pip install locust
- Headless run example: locust -f path/to/task1.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 5m
- The summary prints to console; you can also use --csv=task1 to save CSV report files.

3) If this is an Artillery test
- Ensure artillery installed: npm install -g artillery
- Run: artillery run path/to/task1.yml
- Optional: artillery run --output task1-report.json path/to/task1.yml

4) If this is a JMeter test
- Run in non-GUI: jmeter -n -t path/to/task1.jmx -l task1-results.jtl
- Use the JMeter GUI or third-party tools to convert .jtl to reports.

5) If this is a repo-specific npm script or Docker task
- Check package.json scripts: npm run load:task1 (or similar)
- Or with Docker Compose: docker-compose up --build loadtest-task1
- Or docker run <image> <cmd> (see repo README for exact service name)

What to provide back if you want help interpreting the run
- Console output or saved report (k6 JSON, Locust CSV, Artillery JSON, JMeter .jtl).
- The command you ran and the environment (local machine, CI, Docker).
- Any errors or unexpected behavior.

If you want, paste the exact command you expect me to recommend (tool + path), or paste the test output and I’ll analyze it. If you want me to modify test scripts or CI to run this automatically, submit again with a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
